channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Endorses Andy Barr for Senate as Musk-Backed Candidate Exits Race
item.description: The candidate in Kentucky, Nate Morris, said he would be joining the Trump administration. He met with the president a day before his announcement.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Andy Barr, a Kentucky Republican, was endorsed by President Trump on Friday in the Senate race to succeed Senator Mitch McConnell.

item.title: Georgia Governor Sets Date for Special Election to Fill David Scott’s House Seat
item.description: The winner will serve out the rest of Representative David Scott’s seat in Congress after the longtime lawmaker’s death last month.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
item.media:description: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp speaks during a news conference at the State Capitol last month.

item.title: N.I.H. Reinstates Employee Put on Leave After Criticizing Trump Research Cuts
item.description: Jenna Norton had filed a whistle-blower complaint claiming that the agency leadership had retaliated against her.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:credit: Jose Luis Magana/Associated Press
item.media:description: Jenna Norton in Bethesda, Md., last year.

item.title: Cornell President’s Car Bumps Into Students After Confrontation Over Gaza
item.description: After a debate over the war, students say the university president hit them with his vehicle. He says he was the victim in the incident.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar and Cole Louison
item.media:credit: Students for a Democratic Cornell
item.media:description: Cornell University students videotaped the school’s president as he backed up his vehicle.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Was Proficient in Social Media. So Were His Accusers.
item.description: Eric Swalwell used social media to boost his image and meet women, accusers said. Some of those same women used apps and influencers to join forces years later.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Eric Swalwell in 2019, during a brief run for president. Last month, he left office after sexual misconduct allegations.

item.title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany, Pentagon Says
item.description: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Sean Gallup/Getty Images
item.media:description: Members of the Army participated in a military exercise in Hohenfels, Germany, on Thursday.

item.title: Southern States Move to Redistrict Under Weakened Voting Rights Act
item.description: Republican-led legislatures in Tennessee and Alabama will reconvene in the coming days. Unlike in Tennessee, however, a new map in Alabama will require Supreme Court action.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Julie Bennett/Associated Press
item.media:description: Gov. Kay Ivey of Alabama said on Friday that she would summon lawmakers for a special session to adopt a new congressional map under the newly weakened Voting Rights Act.

item.title: Trump Tells Congress Why He Doesn’t Need Its Authorization for the Iran War
item.description: In letters to the House and Senate, the president asserted that the hostilities had “terminated,” in an apparent attempt to avoid having to seek congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump spoke to reporters before boarding Marine One at the White House on Friday.

item.title: What the Maine Senate Race Feels Like on the Ground
item.description: The anti-establishment mood is palpable in the state, home to one of the country’s top Senate contests. But Senator Susan Collins has some strengths.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times, Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Senator Susan Collins, Gov. Janet Mills and Graham Platner

item.title: Gov. Jeff Landry Signs Law Blocking Ex-Prisoner Calvin Duncan From Taking Office
item.description: Calvin Duncan had been exonerated and elected as criminal court clerk in New Orleans. Gov. Jeff Landry eliminated the role as part of an effort to reshape the city’s judicial system.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Associated Press
item.media:description: Gov. Jeff Landry of Louisiana outside the White House last year.

item.title: Bard College’s President, Leon Botstein, Will Retire After Epstein Revelations
item.description: The president, Leon Botstein, who had run Bard for 50 years, faced scrutiny over his connections to Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Vimal Patel
item.media:credit: Arden Wray for The New York Times
item.media:description: Leon Botstein on the Bard College campus in Annandale-On-Hudson, N.Y.

item.title: Human Remains Found in Tampa Bay Are Identified as Missing Grad Student
item.description: A former University of South Florida student has been charged in the murders of Nahida Bristy and Zamil Limon, doctoral students who were reported missing last month.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:credit: Douglas R. Clifford/Tampa Bay Times, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Investigators in Tampa, Fla., on April 24, after a suspect was arrested in the killings of two University of South Florida doctoral students who had been reported missing.

item.title: Alabama Governor Calls Special Session to Adopt New House Maps
item.description: Gov. Kay Ivey said a map that would give Republicans an additional House seat cannot be enacted without Supreme Court action, but she wants to be ready if that happens.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Julie Bennett/Associated Press
item.media:description: Gov. Kay Ivey of Alabama said on Friday that she would summon lawmakers for a special session to adopt a new congressional map under the newly weakened Voting Rights Act.

item.title: Fact-Checking Claims on Political Violence After Latest Trump Assassination Attempt
item.description: Republican commentators have argued, incorrectly, that political violence is largely a left-wing problem. Liberals have countered by citing the threats made against former President Obama.
item.dc:creator: Linda Qiu
item.category: Center for Strategic and International Studies
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump spoke after a shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner on Saturday. Prosecutors charged the gunman with attempting to assassinate Mr. Trump.

item.title: Secret Service Avoids Hearings for Now on Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting
item.description: Security questions linger nearly a week after a gunman stormed a security checkpoint at the annual dinner.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Nehamas and Eileen Sullivan
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington in April.

item.title: Voters Sue Over Louisiana Governor’s Move to Delay Primary
item.description: Legal challenges are mounting over the decision to suspend the state’s May House primary after a Supreme Court ruling found that the congressional map was unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Hilary Scheinuk/The Advocate, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Gov. Jeff Landry, a Republican from Louisiana, suspended the state’s House primary, which was scheduled for May 16, to draw new congressional districts. Voting rights groups have sued.

item.title: As Iran War Reaches 60-Day Mark, Republicans in Congress Grow Impatient With Trump
item.description: Months into the operation and with midterms looming, some lawmakers are calling for Congress to restrain the president’s war power or set terms for bringing the conflict to a close.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The defense secretary and the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff testified before both chambers of Congress at the Capitol this week.

item.title: May Day Protests Pushing for Worker Protections Expected Across the U.S.
item.description: Organizers have billed the events as a nationwide day of action to demand policies that put the interests of working people over those of the ultrawealthy.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel, Heather Knight, JoAnna Daemmrich and Robert Chiarito
item.media:credit: Jemal Countess/Getty Images for SPACEs in Action
item.media:description: Activists during a rally to observe May Day and D.C. Statehood Day in Washington on Friday.

item.title: Authorities Share New Video of Suspect in White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting
item.description: The F.B.I. and prosecutors shared an annotated video showing the suspect sprinting through a checkpoint and raising a gun. A frame-by-frame analysis suggests that he may have fired.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai, Malachy Browne and Devlin Barrett

item.title: Trump Threatens Higher Tariffs on European Cars
item.description: President Trump said that the European Union was not upholding its part of a trade agreement and that he would increase tariffs next week.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Jeanna Smialek
item.media:credit: Ingmar Nolting for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Volkswagen factory in Zwickau, Germany, in March 2024.

item.title: Jury Convicts Florida Ex-Rep. David Rivera in Conspiracy Trial
item.description: The nation’s state-run oil company hired David Rivera’s consulting firm for $50 million to influence members of Congress and the White House.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams and Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Marco Bello/Reuters
item.media:description: Former U.S. Representative David Rivera at the James Lawrence King Federal Justice Building in Miami in March.

item.title: Florida Redistricts in Republicans’ Favor
item.description: Our politics reporter Nick Corasaniti explains how Florida redrew its congressional district maps to create four more Republican-leaning House seats.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Laura Bult, June Kim and Leanne Abraham

item.title: 5 Victims of Texas Plane Crash Were Traveling to Pickleball Tournament
item.description: Emergency personnel responded to the crash near Wimberley late Thursday night, officials said. The victims were traveling to a pickleball tournament near Austin.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz and Hannah Ziegler
item.media:credit: Jay Janner/Austin American-Statesman, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The crashed Cessna airplane in a wooded area in Wimberley, Texas, on Friday.

item.title: In the Oregon Governor’s Primary, Voters Choose the Future of the Republican Party
item.description: Oregon’s Democratic governor is likely to win re-election, but how voters pick from the ideological array of Republicans challenging her could offer a glimpse at the party post-Trump.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Jordan Gale
item.media:credit: Jordan Gale for The New York Times

item.title: 18-Year-Old Accused of Killing 2 in Kentucky Bank Robbery
item.description: Two employees at a bank in Berea, Ky., were shot and killed. Law enforcement officers arrested the man after a chase, finding a gun in a wrecked BMW.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz
item.media:credit: Kentucky State Police, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The Kentucky State Police released an image of the suspect in a fatal bank robbery on Thursday.

item.title: Pentagon Makes Deals With A.I. Companies to Expand Classified Work
item.description: The agreements with the technology companies come amid the Defense Department’s dispute with Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Sheera Frenkel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Anthropic and the Pentagon have been locked in a debate over whether the company’s A.I. model could be used to pilot autonomous drones or work on domestic surveillance.

item.title: What to Know About the Maine Senate Race After Janet Mills Drops Out
item.description: The exit of Gov. Janet Mills kicked off the general election early in what is likely to be one of the most important, expensive and combative Senate races of 2026.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer and Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times; Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Susan Collins of Maine, a Republican seen as vulnerable given her state’s leftward tilt, is set to face Graham Platner, a progressive oysterman and political newcomer.

item.title: Billionaire Chris Larsen Plans to Spend $3.5 Million in NY House Race Amid Midterm Clash Over A.I.
item.description: Chris Larsen, who hails from California, plans to spend $3.5 million to help Alex Bores, a New York congressional candidate at the center of a proxy war over A.I. regulation.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher and Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Cayce Clifford for The New York Times
item.media:description: The tech billionaire Chris Larsen said that attacks on Alex Bores, a House candidate in New York, by a super PAC aligned with OpenAI were “really despicable.”

item.title: How A.I. Data Centers Are Building a New Political Coalition
item.description: Sabrina Tavernise, a national writer-at-large, traveled to Saline Township, Mich., where the state’s first hyperscale A.I. data center is under construction. Residents are banding together to oppose the center, crossing political lines in surprising new ways.
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise, Mimi Dwyer, Melanie Bencosme, June Kim, Orlando de Guzman and Jon Miller

item.title: ‘The Most Bipartisan Issue Since Beer’: Opposition to Data Centers
item.description: Americans have soured on data centers, polls show, and the sentiment is profoundly bipartisan. How will that change our politics?
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise

item.title: Republicans Want Tennessee’s Last Democratic House District
item.description: Republicans sliced Nashville into three G.O.P.-leaning congressional districts in 2022. After the Supreme Court decision on voting rights, Memphis could be next.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Andrew Harnik/Getty Images
item.media:description: Senator Marsha Blackburn, the favorite to become the state’s next governor, urged the state legislature to quickly adopt a new congressional map putting parts of Memphis, a majority Black city, in Republican hands.
